name: spec-driven-development
description: Forces spec writing before any code on non-trivial tasks

positive_triggers.input: Build a new REST API for managing tasks
positive_triggers.reason: New project, multi-file, needs spec before code

positive_triggers.input: Add a new payment processing flow to the existing app
positive_triggers.reason: Significant new feature, multi-step, needs contract

positive_triggers.input: Refactor the auth module from session-based to OAuth
positive_triggers.reason: Cross-module refactor, behavior change, needs spec

negative_triggers.input: Rename getUserById to fetchUser across the codebase
What does the current spec for the user module say?

behavioral_evals.scenario: User asks: 'Build me a REST API for managing tasks'
expected_behavior: Agent checks OMO plan state (.omo/plans/<id>/plan.md or in-session Prometheus plan)
If no OMO plan exists AND task is non-trivial, guides the user to invoke the Prometheus plan flow (Tab / @plan / /goal) before Phase 2
Agent writes a 'Phase 1: Spec' section into the OMO plan
Agent fills spec template: Goal / Scope / Acceptance Criteria / Commands / Test Strategy / Risks / Risk Review
Agent records L1/L2/L3 as minimum verification strength plus open-world observed/absent/uncertain risk signals
Agent treats listed signals as explicitly non-exhaustive; absence from examples never proves L1
Agent records contract gaps, uncompiled requirements, and uncertainty without inventing thresholds or data shapes
When any signal is observed or uncertain, agent routes the attested Spec to Phase 1.25 contract-strengthening if installed; missing optional extra does not block the core flow
Agent does not treat delegated judgment as approval of unresolved Design or contract choices
Agent writes concrete acceptance criteria (not TBD)
Agent shows spec to user and waits for confirmation before Phase 2

behavioral_evals.scenario: User asks: 'Refactor auth module to use OAuth'
expected_behavior: Agent recognizes cross-module refactor → loads spec-driven-development
Agent lists affected modules in Scope (In/Out)
Agent includes behavior-change acceptance criteria (session-based still works during migration, OAuth works after)
Agent identifies risks (token storage, session migration, backward compat)

notes: Behavioral evals are warning-level until promoted via upstream issue tracker.
